title: From $22k to $138k in 3 years. No options gambling. No "meme stocks." Just math and liquidity cycles.
author: AdForeign7299
selftext:
The 525% return shown in this chart is the inevitable result of treating the market as a calculus problem, rather than a casino.



The logic behind my strategy is simple:



Don't just watch the news; look at the Federal Reserve's balance sheet and credit impulses. I only enter the market when capital is flowing in.



Invest exclusively in companies with exceptionally high ROIC and wide economic moats. If you haven't dissected a company's annual reports for the past five years, don't talk to me about investing.



Don't obsess over RSI and MACD indicators. I focus strictly on Volume Profile and Volatility Skew, lying in wait to ambush positions around the institutional Point of Control (POC)—the zone where institutions established their cost basis.



This approach is incredibly tedious; it offers no dopamine rush—only certainty. You must guard your model like a machine, enduring the solitude of watching gamblers double their money while you wait patiently. But look at my equity curve—it is steady, because mathematics is always more reliable than storytelling.

comments.author: OcelotStraight9145
comments.body: I agree on the thesis. Liquidity cycle correlates to the sp500 performance
